title: R.O.S.S. — AI Companion for Elderly Care
context: H-HACK, H-Farm Robotics Hackathon → H-Farm Venture Building Program
status: Active — in H-Farm Venture Building Program
what_it_is: R.O.S.S. is an AI-powered family companion for the elderly. Not a medical device, not a monitoring system — a domestic presence that accompanies elderly people in their daily routine, remembers with them, and reassures their family through narrative, not surveillance. Built on a local-first AI architecture with a biographical knowledge base.
core_insight: The eldercare market is saturated with products that treat the elderly as patients to monitor. ROSS exists because there's another possibility: the elderly person as someone to accompany. This distinction is not marketing — it's the foundation of the entire product, regulatory positioning (AI Act Art. 50, non-MDR), and IP strategy.

three_presence_modes.active: ROSS moves toward Maria with narrative intent — guided by episodic memory. It approaches because it has something to share or remember with her. No biometric triggers, no surveillance.
three_presence_modes.silent: ROSS detects anomalous absence (from voice interaction absence, not biometric sensors) and sends Marco a narrative sentence, not an alert. Discretion, not alarm.
three_presence_modes.reactive: ROSS responds when Maria calls it. Availability, not automatism.

key_features: F1: Biographically personalised conversation (using ontologia biografica)
F2: Real-time conversational adaptation (ISCS — Indice di Stato Cognitivo di Sessione)
F4: Adaptive mnemonic games (built on biography, not generic exercises)
F5: Portfolio of 6 vocal interactive games (biographically personalised)
F9: Emergent interest detection (from conversation patterns)
F11: Daily Report — narrative letter to caregiver (not a dashboard)
F13: Caregiver mobile app (onboarding, Daily Report, consent management)
F14: Movement with narrative intent (deferred post-2027, IP timing)
F15: Physical device form factor (design patent in progress)

technical_architecture.core: On-device AI — local-first architecture. Everything stays on Maria's device.
technical_architecture.ai_stack: Small language model (optimized/quantized, LoRA/PEFT adaptation), speech-to-text, text-to-speech, all local
technical_architecture.key_component: Ontologia biografica — structured biographical knowledge base (people, places, events, routines, preferences, memories of Maria)
technical_architecture.cloud: Only the text of the Daily Report (with explicit double consent) — never audio, transcripts, or raw data
technical_architecture.privacy: GDPR Art. 9 compliant. Maria can delete all her data with a single voice command.
technical_architecture.tools_mentioned: Voiceflow (for dialogue prototyping), Whisper + TTS pipeline

regulatory_positioning.ai_act: Art. 50 — limited risk (not high risk). Mandatory AI disclosure to Maria in onboarding.
regulatory_positioning.mdr: Non-MDR — deliberately and actively maintained. No biometric sensors, no clinical claims, no therapeutic monitoring.
regulatory_positioning.gdpr: Art. 9 data (voice as special category). DPIA required before any pilot with real users. Double explicit consent (Maria + Marco separately).
regulatory_positioning.glossary_constraint: Banned words in all product, code, and comms: paziente (patient), terapia (therapy), robot, monitoraggio (monitoring), controllo (control). Required words: accompagna, rassicura, ricorda, compagno, presenza, routine.

ip_strategy.patent_1: Conversational layer — covers F1-F10. Must be filed before any public demo of technical details.
ip_strategy.patent_2: NLG for caregiver, app, hardware (F11, F12, F13, F15) — in development.
ip_strategy.deferred: Navigation patent (F14) deferred post-2027 due to AVA Robotics patents expiring 2026-2027.
ip_strategy.trademark: EUIPO/UIBM in classes 9, 28, 42, 44 — explicitly NOT class 10 (medical devices).

market.pricing_hypothesis: €1,000 device + €40/month subscription (~€1,480 first year = less than one month of a live-in carer at €1,400/month)
market.buyer: Marco — adult child 40-60, not Maria herself. Purchase trigger: specific incident (fall, disorientation, worrying call).
market.competitors: ElliQ (cloud-dependent, US only), Paro (no cognitive function), SOS buttons (passive), Apple Watch (requires wearing)
market.gap: No consumer product combines physical presence + zero-friction interface for elderly + narrative reassurance for caregiver.

team.federico: Finance & Commercial Development — financial models, investor relations (angel/VC/accelerators), unit economics, projections, cap table, fundraising rounds, pricing (D-05 owner), WTP research (G-01 owner)
team.alessandro: Product, Positioning, Communication — go-to-market, pitch, brand, commercial partners
team.alessandra: Institutional Relations & Operations — medical gatekeepers, RSA partnerships, caregiver relations, roadmap/backlog
team.luca: Architecture & Technical Development — AI stack, on-device, GDPR compliance, tech team
team.incoming: AI Full Stack Engineer (freelance ~6mo, potential equity) — prototyping, backend, agentic systems, voice layer

current_status.stage: Post-hackathon, in H-Farm Venture Building Program
current_status.recognition: Selected top project at H-HACK (H-Farm Robotics Hackathon)
current_status.patent: Patent application in progress (Art. 56 CPI provisional deposit)
current_status.open_decisions: Form factor (stationary vs walker), SoC selection, DPO engagement, legal counsel engagement, pricing validation
current_status.next_milestone: Patent deposit → trademark clearance → DPIA → pilot with real users

federico_specific_contributions: Financial model and unit economics (pricing hypothesis, TAM/SAM/SOM)
